---
title: "Description of BioHUG"
editor: visual
---

BioHUG was set up in 2024 and started banking DNA samples from patients who have signed the general consent. It was approved by the HUG Directorate and supported by the Faculty of Medicine. The HUG informatics team set up a system that produces daily lists of blood samples that come from patients who have signed the general consent. These lists are processed 2 or 3 times a week to ensure no duplication. Our laboratory technician, Mr Tiago De Sousa, from Medical Genetics takes the list and picks up spare blood samples from haematology in batches of 48. Tiago then checks the samples are not clotted and have at least 1.5mls of blood left before extracting DNA in the HUG medical genetics laboratory. Most weeks he processes 200-250 blood samples. In summer 2025 ethics permission was obtained to link to research studies and prioritise samples from research studies. If you have an existing research study or patient registry, or would like to set up a research study, and are interested in banking DNA in a cost efficient manner please contact us at Timothy.frayling\@unige.ch. If you wish to collect serum and plasma this may also be possible. For more details see the [How to Use](howtouse.qmd) page.

![The above diagram illustrates how samples are intercepted](../images/BioHUG_2026_outline.png){fig-align="center" width="75%"}
